summary:
KEY FUNCTIONALITY:
-Aggregates a sequence of double values.

CORE LOGIC:
-Sums up the double values using the addNextValue method.
-Returns the string representation of the aggregated value using the getReport method.
-Resets the aggregator to its initial state using the reset method.
-Returns an array of one element containing the string representation of the aggregated value using the getCombinerOutput method.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:
-Accepts a double value or a string representation of a double value using the addNextValue method.
-Returns the string representation of the aggregated value using the getReport method.

DEPENDENCIES:
-None.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY: Maintains the maximum of a sequence of long values.
CORE LOGIC: The addNextValue method updates the maximum value if a new value is greater than the current maximum. The reset method resets the maximum value to the minimum value.
INPUTS/OUTPUTS: The addNextValue method takes a long value or a string representation of a long value. The getReport method returns the string representation of the maximum value. The getCombinerOutput method returns an array of one element, which is the string representation of the maximum value.
DEPENDENCIES: None.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY: Maintains the minimum of a sequence of long values.
CORE LOGIC: Key algorithms or processing patterns visible in this chunk are maintaining the minimum value.
INPUTS/OUTPUTS: Data flowing in and out includes adding values to the aggregator and getting the aggregated value.
DEPENDENCIES: Key imported, parent, or collaborator classes visible in this chunk are not explicitly mentioned, but the code is part of the Apache Hadoop MapReduce library.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:
-Aggregates a sequence of long values.

CORE LOGIC:
-Sums up a sequence of long values.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:
-Input: a sequence of long values.
-Output: the sum of the input values.

DEPENDENCIES:
-None.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY: Maintains the biggest of a sequence of strings.
CORE LOGIC: Compares each new string value to the current maximum value and updates the maximum if the new value is greater.
INPUTS/OUTPUTS: Input is a string value, output is the maximum string value seen so far.
DEPENDENCIES: None.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:
-Aggregates the smallest string value from a sequence of strings.

CORE LOGIC:
-Keeps track of the smallest string value seen so far.
-Updates the minimum value when a new value is added.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:
-Input: a string value to be added to the aggregation.
-Output: the smallest string value seen so far.

DEPENDENCIES:
-None.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:
- The main purpose of this code is to implement a value aggregator that deduplicates a sequence of objects.

CORE LOGIC:
- The code uses a TreeMap to store unique objects and their counts.
- It also keeps track of the total number of items and the maximum number of unique values allowed.
- The addNextValue method adds a value to the aggregator, and the getCombinerOutput method returns an array of the unique objects.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:
- The code takes in a sequence of objects and returns the number of unique objects.

DEPENDENCIES:
- The code depends on the org.apache.hadoop.mapreduce.lib.aggregate package.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:
- Creates an object of ValueAggregatorDescriptor from the name of a user-defined class that may be dynamically loaded.
- Delegates invocations of the generateKeyValPairs function to the created object.

CORE LOGIC:
- Creates an instance of the given class using reflection.
- Configures the created object.
- Generates a list of aggregation-id/value pairs for the given key/value pairs by delegating the invocation to the real object.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:
- The class name of the user-defined descriptor class.
- A configuration object used for descriptor configuration.

DEPENDENCIES:
- org.apache.hadoop.classification.InterfaceAudience
- org.apache.hadoop.classification.InterfaceStability
- org.apache.hadoop.conf.Configuration
- org.apache.hadoop.io.Text

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:
- This interface defines the minimal protocol for value aggregators.

CORE LOGIC:
- The addNextValue method adds a value to the aggregator.
- The reset method resets the aggregator.
- The getReport method returns the string representation of the aggregator.
- The getCombinerOutput method returns an array of values as the outputs of the combiner.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:
- The addNextValue method takes an Object as input and adds it to the aggregator.
- The reset method does not take any input and simply resets the aggregator.
- The getReport method does not take any input and returns the string representation of the aggregator.
- The getCombinerOutput method does not take any input and returns an array of values as the outputs of the combiner.

DEPENDENCIES:
- This interface does not import any other classes.
- It is not a child or collaborator of any other classes.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:
-Aggregates values based on their type and id
-Generates key/value pairs for each input key/value pair
-Counts the total number of records in the input data and the number of records in each input file

CORE LOGIC:
-Generates aggregation id/value pairs for each input key/value pair
-Aggregates values based on their type and id

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:
-Input key/value pairs
-Aggregation id/value pairs

DEPENDENCIES:
-None

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:
The main purpose of this chunk is to implement the generic combiner of Aggregate.

CORE LOGIC:
The code uses the ValueAggregatorDescriptor to determine the type of aggregation to perform. It then uses the ValueAggregatorBaseDescriptor to generate a ValueAggregator object. The object is used to add the values and then get the combined output.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:
The input is a key-value pair, where the key is a Text object and the value is also a Text object. The output is also a key-value pair, where the key is the same as the input key and the value is the combined output.

DEPENDENCIES:
The class depends on the ValueAggregatorDescriptor, ValueAggregatorBaseDescriptor, and the Reducer class from the Apache Hadoop MapReduce library.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:
- Generate a list of aggregation-id/value pairs for the given key/value pair
- Guide the way to aggregate the value in the reduce/combiner phrase of an Aggregate based job

CORE LOGIC:
- Create aggregation id/value pairs

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:
- Input key
- Input value
- Aggregation id/value pairs

DEPENDENCIES:
- None

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:

The main purpose of this chunk of code is to create a map/reduce job using the Aggregate framework.

CORE LOGIC:

The code provides a set of built-in value aggregators, including sum, count, histogram, minimum, maximum, median, average, and standard deviation. It also provides a generic utility class that helps users create map/reduce jobs using the generic mapper/reducer/combiner classes.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:

The code takes input from a specified directory, using either the TextInputFormat or SequenceFileInputFormat. It outputs the results to a specified directory using the TextOutputFormat.

DEPENDENCIES:

The code depends on the Apache Hadoop MapReduce framework and the Aggregate library. It also depends on the GenericOptionsParser class for parsing generic Hadoop arguments.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:
This code chunk is part of the ValueAggregatorJobBase class, which is used as a base class for MapReduce jobs that use the ValueAggregator framework. It provides functionality for setting up the job, initializing the aggregator descriptors, and logging the aggregator specifications.

CORE LOGIC:
The code chunk does not contain any specific algorithms or processing patterns.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:
The code chunk does not specify any specific inputs or outputs.

DEPENDENCIES:
The code chunk depends on the ValueAggregatorDescriptor class and the UserDefinedValueAggregatorDescriptor class. It also depends on the Configuration class from the Hadoop framework.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:
The main purpose of this chunk of code is to implement the generic mapper of Aggregate.

CORE LOGIC:
The code iterates through the value aggregator descriptor list to generate aggregation id/value pairs and emit them.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:
The code takes in a key and value of type K1 and V1 respectively, and emits Text keys and values.

DEPENDENCIES:
The code depends on the ValueAggregatorJobBase class and its setup method, as well as the ValueAggregatorDescriptor class for aggregation logic.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:
The main purpose of this chunk is to implement the generic reducer of Aggregate.

CORE LOGIC:
The key algorithms or processing patterns visible in this chunk are:
- The setup method is called to set up the context for the reducer.
- The reduce method is called for each key-value pair in the input, where the key is a Text object indicating the type of aggregation to perform, and the values are the data to be aggregated.
- The reduce method extracts the type of aggregation from the key, creates a ValueAggregator object of the appropriate type, and adds each value to the aggregator.
- The reduce method then writes the aggregated value to the output using the original key.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:
The data flowing in and out of this chunk is:
- The input consists of key-value pairs, where the key is a Text object and the value is a Text object.
- The output consists of key-value pairs, where the key is the original key and the value is the aggregated value as a Text object.

DEPENDENCIES:
The key imported, parent, or collaborator classes visible in this chunk are:
- ValueAggregatorJobBase
- ValueAggregatorDescriptor
- UniqValueCount
- ValueAggregatorBaseDescriptor
- ValueAggregator
- Text
- Writable
- WritableComparable
- Reducer
- Context
- IOException
- InterruptedException

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:
- Computes the histogram of a sequence of strings

CORE LOGIC:
- Maintains a TreeMap of values and their frequencies
- addNextValue() method adds a value to the aggregator, incrementing its frequency
- getReport() method returns a string representation of the histogram, including basic statistics
- getReportDetails() method returns a string representation of the list of value/frequency pairs
- getCombinerOutput() method returns a list of value/frequency pairs for use by the reducer
- reset() method resets the aggregator

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:
- addNextValue() method takes a string in the form of xxxx\tnum, where xxxx is a value and num is its frequency
- getReport() method returns a string representation of the histogram
- getReportDetails() method returns a string representation of the list of value/frequency pairs
- getCombinerOutput() method returns a list of value/frequency pairs

DEPENDENCIES:
- None
